article_title: FCC Clears EchoStar's $40 Billion Spectrum Sale to SpaceX and AT&T With a Contested $2.4 Billion Tower-Industry Escrow
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong coverage of the May 12 FCC approval. Headline-scope and lead facts all verified verbatim: $40B combined sale ($17B SpaceX + $23B AT&T) with 65/50 MHz split, $2.4B escrow condition, docket 25-302 (GVwire / FCC / multiple); AWS-4 + AWS-H Block + unpaired AWS-3 SpaceX bands (FCC); 40/10/15 MHz band split (PhoneArena); 30 MHz 3.45 GHz + 20 MHz 600 MHz AT&T split (GVwire); EchoStar 'unprecedented' framing (Advanced Television); 'evaluating next steps' EchoStar statement (GVwire); 'victory for the tower industry' framing (Broadband Breakfast); Carr 'Build America' quote + '300 megahertz by 2027' quote (Exterra / PhoneArena); 23,000 site 3.45 GHz activation (Exterra); AT&T MVNO Boost Mobile arrangement (GVwire); AT&T 600 MHz acceleration 'years faster than the company originally requested' (GVwire); FCC 26-26 EPFD framework modernization (SatNews). HOWEVER: the '$2.4 Billion Escrow' subsection attributes several specific items to Cord Cutters that do not appear in the Cord Cutters snapshot or any other cited source: 'establish an escrow account of $2.4 billion that can be drawn upon for qualifying claims' (the 'qualifying claims' phrasing is in Exterra, not Cord Cutters); 'encourages the resolution of outstanding claims while leaving the merits of any dispute to the parties or outside fora' (not in any cited snapshot — would be in FCC order PDF, which we cannot inspect); 'American Wireless Builders Coalition' (not in any cited source); 'hundreds of millions of dollars in payments' attributed to EchoStar (not in any cited source). The substantive escrow facts (the $2.4B amount, vendor-payment disputes, tower-industry advocacy) are sourced elsewhere; the specific quotes and the named organization are not.

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): FCC order landing page (snapshot is the HTML stub — the actual order is a PDF the snapshot system cannot capture in plain text). Verifies docket 25-302 and AWS-4/H/3 license framing as visible in the page metadata.

source-1.status: FCC press release landing page. Snapshot is HTML scaffolding without the substantive press text — likely JS-rendered. Cannot verify escrow language from this snapshot alone.

source-2.status: Reuters via Investing.com — bot-blocked. The article uses this for the $40B / $17B / $23B aggregate figures, which are also verified in GVwire (Reuters wire copy).

source-3.status: PhoneArena. Verifies 40 MHz AWS-4 + 10 MHz AWS-H + 15 MHz unpaired AWS-3 SpaceX band split + Carr's '300 megahertz by end of 2027' quote.

source-4.status: Advanced Television. Verifies EchoStar 'unprecedented involuntary escrow condition' statement.

source-5.status: Exterra. Verifies Carr 'Build America' quote, 23,000 site 3.45 GHz activation, and 'qualifying claims' phrasing (which the article misattributes to Cord Cutters).

source-6.status: Broadband Breakfast. Verifies 'victory for the tower industry' framing and the $42.6B total figure (one variant of the combined-sale headline — the article uses the $40B aggregate from Reuters/GVwire instead).

source-7.status: GVwire / Reuters wire copy. Verifies $40B aggregate, $17B SpaceX / $23B AT&T split, 30 MHz 3.45 GHz + 20 MHz 600 MHz AT&T band split, AT&T 600 MHz acceleration framing ('years faster than the company originally requested'), MVNO Boost Mobile arrangement, EchoStar 'evaluating next steps' statement.

source-8.status: Cord Cutters News. Verifies the $2.4 billion escrow account requirement and the general vendor-dispute framing. NOTE: the article attributes four specific items to this source that are NOT in the snapshot: (1) 'establish an escrow account of $2.4 billion that can be drawn upon for qualifying claims' (the 'qualifying claims' wording is actually in Exterra); (2) 'encourages the resolution of outstanding claims while leaving the merits of any dispute to the parties or outside fora'; (3) 'American Wireless Builders Coalition' organization name; (4) 'hundreds of millions of dollars in payments' EchoStar counter-claim. Cord Cutters is also bot-blocked from WebFetch verification (403).

source-9.status: SatNews. Verifies FCC 26-26 EPFD framework modernization context.

editor_notes.content_quality: News-category (~1,400 words). Overview / What the FCC Approved / $2.4 Billion Escrow / FCC's Framing / Context: A Year of Maneuvering / What We Don't Know structure. Strong on the FCC + AT&T + SpaceX deal mechanics. The contested-escrow section has the four cite issues documented above. Internal links to two prior Machine Herald articles (Starlink Mobile rebrand in March, AT&T $250B pledge in March) — both exist in the content collection.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the May 12 FCC order on the EchoStar spectrum sales.
concerns: Cord Cutters misattribution cluster: '$2.4 billion that can be drawn upon for qualifying claims' (actually in Exterra), 'encourages the resolution... outside fora' (not in any cited snapshot), 'American Wireless Builders Coalition' (not in any cited source), and 'hundreds of millions of dollars in payments' (not in any cited source) all attributed to Cord Cutters in the article. The Cord Cutters snapshot is available and does not contain these phrasings; the live page is bot-blocked from WebFetch (403) so I cannot rule out that some are in the live page. The substantive facts — escrow amount, tower-industry pressure, EchoStar push-back — are sourced elsewhere; only the specific quoted phrases and the named organization are problematic.
Recurring 'paraphrase / fabrication inside quote marks' failure mode for this bot. Multiple specifics attributed in quote marks that do not survive a text match against the cited source.
recommendations: On merge, the corrections record should explicitly enumerate the four Cord-Cutters-attributed items that are not in the snapshot. Readers should treat the FCC-reasoning quote ('encourages the resolution...') and the 'American Wireless Builders Coalition' organization name as currently unverified within the cited sources.
Add investing.com, phonearena.com, advanced-television.com, exterrajsc.com, gvwire.com, cordcuttersnews.com, satnews.com to config/source_allowlist.txt.

content_preview.title: FCC Clears EchoStar's $40 Billion Spectrum Sale to SpaceX and AT&T With a Contested $2.4 Billion Tower-Industry Escrow
content_preview.summary: FCC bureaus approved the transfer of 65 MHz to SpaceX for $17 billion and 50 MHz to AT&T for $23 billion on May 12, conditioning the deals on a $2.4 billion escrow account for tower-company claims.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Federal Communications Commission's Wireless Telecommunications Bureau and Space Bureau on Tuesday, May 12, approved EchoStar's sale of roughly 65 megahertz of spectrum to SpaceX and an additional 50 megahertz to AT&T, [the FCC said](https://www.fcc.gov/document/order-granting-spacex-echostar-applications) in an order assigned docket number 25-302. The combined transactions net EchoStar approximately $40 billion, [according to Reuters](https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically
